# 🗂️ Data (111)

Models are only as good as what you feed them, and this is where the feedstock gets made. From pipelines that move terabytes to synthetic generators and annotation toolkits, here's everything for turning raw chaos into training-grade fuel.

[↑ Back to index](../README.md)

## ETL/Pipelines

Move, transform, and orchestrate data at scale so your models never starve mid-train

<details>
<summary>Show 29 repos</summary>

- [Zie619/n8n-workflows](https://github.com/Zie619/n8n-workflows) — ⭐ 55,100 · `Python` · Dataset — A collection of over 4,000 pre-built n8n workflows covering 365 integrations, designed to automate various tasks and data flows. It provides a comprehensive set of examples and configurations for using the n8n automation platform.
- [huggingface/datasets](https://github.com/huggingface/datasets) — ⭐ 21,533 · `Python` · Library — A Python library that provides a vast hub of ready-to-use datasets for AI models and offers fast, efficient tools for data manipulation, preprocessing, cleaning, and annotation of text, image, and audio data.
- [Avaiga/taipy](https://github.com/Avaiga/taipy) — ⭐ 19,254 · `Python` · Framework — Taipy is a Python-based open-source framework that enables data scientists and AI developers to quickly build and deploy production-ready web applications from their data and AI algorithms without needing frontend expertise. It handles backend deployment, data flow, scheduling, and provides built-in UI and data visualization components, making it ideal for creating interactive dashboards and applications around AI scripts and large language models.
- [Unstructured-IO/unstructured](https://github.com/Unstructured-IO/unstructured) — ⭐ 15,110 · `HTML` · Library — Unstructured is an open-source ETL solution designed to convert complex, unstructured documents into clean, structured data, making it suitable for processing by large language models. It supports partitioning, enrichment, chunking, and embedding.
- [run-llama/liteparse](https://github.com/run-llama/liteparse) — ⭐ 7,941 · `Rust` · Library — Liteparse is a fast, open-source document parser written in Rust, designed for efficient extraction of text and data from various document formats like PDFs, and includes support for OCR.
- [Eventual-Inc/Daft](https://github.com/Eventual-Inc/Daft) — ⭐ 5,507 · `Rust` · Framework — This is a high-performance data engine designed for AI and multimodal workloads. It processes structured data, images, audio, and video at any scale, facilitating data engineering and machine learning pipelines.
- [business-science/ai-data-science-team](https://github.com/business-science/ai-data-science-team) — ⭐ 5,236 · `Python` · Application — This Python project provides an AI-powered data science team of agents designed to automate and accelerate common data science tasks such as data loading, cleaning, exploratory analysis, and feature engineering, creating reproducible pipelines.
- [camelot-dev/camelot](https://github.com/camelot-dev/camelot) — ⭐ 3,704 · `Python` · Library — A Python library designed to extract tabular data from PDF documents, allowing export into formats like CSV, JSON, Excel, or HTML.
- [OpenDCAI/DataFlow](https://github.com/OpenDCAI/DataFlow) — ⭐ 3,561 · `Python` · Application — This tool simplifies data preparation for LLMs, enabling the generation and processing of LLM-ready datasets from raw data using pipelines and operators. It focuses on cleaning, synthesis, and general data processing tasks for AI applications.
- [huggingface/datatrove](https://github.com/huggingface/datatrove) — ⭐ 3,159 · `Python` · Library — Datatrove is a Python library that provides platform-agnostic, customizable processing blocks for building large-scale data pipelines. It aims to simplify data preparation and cleaning, particularly for machine learning datasets, by offering modular components for tasks like I/O and transformation.
- [datachain-ai/datachain](https://github.com/datachain-ai/datachain) — ⭐ 2,754 · `Python` · Library — This Python library provides a context layer for managing and versioning unstructured data, such as PDFs and images, stored in cloud services like S3, GCS, and Azure. It makes these datasets traceable, queryable, and reusable for applications like RAG and AI agents.
- [ShinMegamiBoson/OpenPlanter](https://github.com/ShinMegamiBoson/OpenPlanter) — ⭐ 1,602 · `Python` · AI Agent — An open-source project developing a recursive agent capable of resolving entities across various corporate registries, likely for data reconciliation or compliance.
- [NVIDIA-NeMo/Curator](https://github.com/NVIDIA-NeMo/Curator) — ⭐ 1,575 · `Python` · Library — A scalable toolkit for preprocessing and curating large-scale datasets, specifically designed to enhance the quality of data used for training and fine-tuning Large Language Models.
- [KnugiHK/WhatsApp-Chat-Exporter](https://github.com/KnugiHK/WhatsApp-Chat-Exporter) — ⭐ 1,064 · `Python` · CLI Tool — This is a customizable, cross-platform CLI tool for parsing WhatsApp chat databases from Android and iOS/iPadOS backups. It supports various encryption formats and outputs the chat history in readable HTML or structured JSON.
- [mosaico-labs/mosaico](https://github.com/mosaico-labs/mosaico) — ⭐ 861 · `Python` · Self-hosted Service — Mosaico is a data platform built for Physical AI applications, providing a structured archive for sensor logs collected from robotic systems.
- [PacktPublishing/Data-Engineering-with-Python](https://github.com/PacktPublishing/Data-Engineering-with-Python) — ⭐ 797 · `Python` · Tutorial/Course — This repository provides code and resources accompanying a Packt book on data engineering with Python. It teaches how to build large-scale data pipelines using Python.
- [0xSero/ai-data-extraction](https://github.com/0xSero/ai-data-extraction) — ⭐ 675 · `Python` · CLI Tool — This toolkit extracts personal data and full conversation histories, including code context and tool call results, from various AI coding assistants like Cursor, Claude Code, and Codex. The extracted data is output in a unified JSONL format, suitable for tasks like model fine-tuning.
- [Kaelio/ktx-ai-data-agents-mcp-context-skills](https://github.com/Kaelio/ktx-ai-data-agents-mcp-context-skills) — ⭐ 614 · `TypeScript` · MCP Server — ktx is an executable context layer designed for AI data and analytics agents. It enables tools like Claude Code and Codex to accurately query data warehouses by providing skills, memory, and a semantic layer derived from business knowledge sources.
- [AltimateAI/altimate-code](https://github.com/AltimateAI/altimate-code) — ⭐ 577 · `TypeScript` · Framework — This is an open-source agentic data engineering framework designed for dbt, SQL, and various cloud data warehouses, providing tools for SQL analysis, column-level lineage tracking, and schema indexing, specifically to enhance LLM agents with accurate data engineering capabilities.
- [simplifaisoul/osiris](https://github.com/simplifaisoul/osiris) — ⭐ 386 · `TypeScript` · Self-hosted Service — Osiris is an open-source global intelligence platform providing a real-time OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) dashboard. It aims to be an alternative to commercial intelligence tools like Palantir, focusing on data collection, processing, and visualization for intelligence analysis.
- [marciok/gust](https://github.com/marciok/gust) — ⭐ 330 · `Elixir` · Framework — Gust is a task-based workflow orchestration engine designed to automate data processing and machine learning pipelines, featuring dynamic conditional execution and an API for creating runs.
- [opengeos/qgis-gee-data-catalogs-plugin](https://github.com/opengeos/qgis-gee-data-catalogs-plugin) — ⭐ 287 · `Python` · Plugin/Extension — This is a QGIS plugin that enables users to search for and access data from both the official Earth Engine Data Catalog and the Awesome GEE Community Catalog. It facilitates the integration of geospatial data into QGIS for visualization and analysis, with potential for AI-assisted workflows.
- [sbroenne/mcp-server-excel](https://github.com/sbroenne/mcp-server-excel) — ⭐ 262 · `C#` · MCP Server — This project provides an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server and CLI tool for AI-powered automation of Microsoft Excel. It offers 23 tools and 214 operations to control Excel via its COM API, enabling natural language interaction with features like Power Query, DAX, and PivotTables.
- [OpenStrap/edge](https://github.com/OpenStrap/edge) — ⭐ 59 · `Dart` · Application — This Flutter application reverse-engineers the WHOOP 4.0's Bluetooth protocol to drain raw data from the wearable band. It allows users to sync this data to their own backend, providing insight into the strap's actual measurements.
- [sjtu-sai-agents/DataMaster](https://github.com/sjtu-sai-agents/DataMaster) — ⭐ 34 · `Python` · AI Agent — DataMaster is an autonomous agent designed to improve AI model performance by addressing data quality issues through data engineering. It uses a tree-search approach to discover and integrate external datasets.
- [macrodata-labs/refiner](https://github.com/macrodata-labs/refiner) — ⭐ 21 · `Python` · Framework — Refiner is a data processing framework by Macrodata Labs, designed for large-scale machine learning datasets.
- [DenisSergeevitch/UFO-USA](https://github.com/DenisSergeevitch/UFO-USA) — ⭐ 20 · `Python` · Library — A project that converts raw, blurry PDF data from the Dept of War's UAP archive into a searchable Markdown format. It creates an LLM-friendly index, making UFO encounter records easily consumable by AI models.
- [oak-invest/kiso](https://github.com/oak-invest/kiso) — ⭐ 12 · `Java` · Self-hosted Service — A publishing engine that converts Open Knowledge Format (OKF) bundles into static websites, designed for both human readers and AI agents.
- [run-llama/parse-flow](https://github.com/run-llama/parse-flow) — ⭐ 10 · `TypeScript` · Application — A visual flow editor designed to help users configure and manage data parsing workflows, specifically for extracting structured information from unstructured documents like PDFs using LlamaParse for AI pipelines.

</details>

## Synthetic Data

Generate training data out of thin air when the real thing is scarce, private, or expensive

- [faker-js/faker](https://github.com/faker-js/faker) — ⭐ 15,035 · `TypeScript` · Library — Faker.js is a library designed to generate large quantities of realistic fake data for testing and development purposes, working in both browser and Node.js environments.
- [faker-ruby/faker](https://github.com/faker-ruby/faker) — ⭐ 11,734 · `Ruby` · Library — Faker is a Ruby library designed to generate various types of realistic-looking fake data, including names, addresses, and phone numbers, useful for testing and development.
- [InternScience/GraphGen](https://github.com/InternScience/GraphGen) — ⭐ 1,020 · `Python` · CLI Tool — This tool generates synthetic training data for large language models (LLMs) by leveraging knowledge graphs, aiming to enhance supervised fine-tuning for tasks like question answering.
- [intellicia-public/parastore](https://github.com/intellicia-public/parastore) — ⭐ 524 · `TypeScript` · Application — ParaStore is an isometric 3D sandbox application where users can design stores, generate LLM-powered virtual consumer personas, and observe their shopping behavior. It's designed for synthetic-consumer experiments and agent simulation in a retail context.
- [weikaih04/Synthetic-Detection-Segmentation-Grounding-Data](https://github.com/weikaih04/Synthetic-Detection-Segmentation-Grounding-Data) — ⭐ 47 · `Python` · Dataset — An accurate and densely-annotated synthetic dataset designed for training state-of-the-art object detectors, segmentors, and Grounding-VLMs.
- [NVIDIA-NeMo/Safe-Synthesizer](https://github.com/NVIDIA-NeMo/Safe-Synthesizer) — ⭐ 22 · `Python` · Library — This tool, part of the NeMo framework, creates private and safe synthetic versions of sensitive tabular datasets. It also evaluates the quality and privacy of these synthetic datasets by comparing distributions and running membership/attribute inference attacks.
- [xlang-ai/CUA-Gym](https://github.com/xlang-ai/CUA-Gym) — ⭐ 14 · `Python` · Framework — CUA-Gym is a scalable pipeline and synthesis engine designed to generate verifiable RLVR (Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards) training data specifically for computer-use agents.

## Annotation

Label faster with human-in-the-loop tooling, active learning, and LLM-assisted tagging

- [wkentaro/labelme](https://github.com/wkentaro/labelme) — ⭐ 15,834 · `Python` · Application — Labelme is a Python-based application for annotating images and videos, supporting various annotation shapes with capabilities for AI-assisted labeling. It is primarily used for creating labeled datasets for computer vision and deep learning tasks.
- [HumanSignal/Adala](https://github.com/HumanSignal/Adala) — ⭐ 1,427 · `Python` · Agent Framework — Adala is a framework for autonomous data labeling agents, designed to automate the process of annotating data.
- [simoncirstoiu/alice](https://github.com/simoncirstoiu/alice) — ⭐ 301 · `JavaScript` · Application — Alice is an AI-powered toolkit for managing YOLO datasets, supporting analysis, ingestion, curation, and export. It assists in creating datasets for object detection and computer vision training.

## Quality/Validation

Catch leaks, dupes, and garbage rows before they poison your dataset

- [apache/ossie](https://github.com/apache/ossie) — ⭐ 1,159 · `Python` · Framework — Apache Ossie is an industry-wide specification and framework for standardizing the exchange of semantic metadata across analytics, AI, and BI platforms. It aims to provide a vendor-neutral, single source of truth for semantic data, preventing inconsistencies and enabling seamless, version-controlled data flow.
- [Kaelio/ktx](https://github.com/Kaelio/ktx) — ⭐ 222 · `TypeScript` · Library — This repository provides an executable context layer for analytics and data agents, aiming to improve their reliability and ensure the generation of correct SQL by combining wiki-style context with semantic-layer reliability.
- [tinyfish-io/bigset](https://github.com/tinyfish-io/bigset) — ⭐ 57 · `TypeScript` · Self-hosted Service — An open-source system designed for querying and managing massive datasets. It focuses on retrieving accurate information and preventing 'hallucinations' often encountered when retrieving data from large systems, offering an API for integration.
- [jackvial/lerobot-data-studio](https://github.com/jackvial/lerobot-data-studio) — ⭐ 50 · `TypeScript` · Application — This is an unofficial web-based GUI designed for editing and cleaning datasets specifically used in LeRobot robotics projects, supporting applications such as behavioral cloning and embodied AI.
- [QwenLM/Qwen-Image-Bench](https://github.com/QwenLM/Qwen-Image-Bench) — ⭐ 21 · `Python` · Dataset — This repository provides 'Qwen-Image-Bench', a benchmark dataset likely used for evaluating and validating the performance of image-related AI models, as indicated by its presence on Hugging Face and ModelScope datasets.
- [meituan-longcat/VitaBench-2.0](https://github.com/meituan-longcat/VitaBench-2.0) — ⭐ 18 · `Python` · Dataset — VitaBench 2.0 is a benchmark dataset and evaluation suite designed to measure the ability of AI models to understand and align with human preferences, highlighting where current models fall short in avoiding 'preference errors'.

## Web Crawling

Scrape, harvest, and clean the open web into structured, model-ready corpora

<details>
<summary>Show 24 repos</summary>

- [unclecode/crawl4ai](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai) — ⭐ 66,219 · `Python` · Application — Crawl4AI is an open-source, LLM-friendly web crawler and scraper designed to efficiently extract data from websites. It focuses on providing robust web scraping capabilities, especially for AI applications.
- [scrapy/scrapy](https://github.com/scrapy/scrapy) — ⭐ 61,813 · `Python` · Framework — Scrapy is a fast, high-level web crawling and scraping framework built with Python. It provides a comprehensive set of tools for efficiently extracting structured data from the web.
- [ScrapeGraphAI/Scrapegraph-ai](https://github.com/ScrapeGraphAI/Scrapegraph-ai) — ⭐ 26,015 · `Python` · Library — ScrapeGraphAI is a Python-based web scraper that leverages AI, including Large Language Models and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) techniques, for intelligent data extraction. It acts as an AI-powered crawler for obtaining web data.
- [projectdiscovery/katana](https://github.com/projectdiscovery/katana) — ⭐ 16,756 · `Go` · Framework — Katana is a next-generation crawling and spidering framework written in Go, designed for efficient web content discovery and enumeration. It functions as a command-line tool for reconnaissance.
- [getmaxun/maxun](https://github.com/getmaxun/maxun) — ⭐ 15,613 · `TypeScript` · Self-hosted Service — Maxun is an open-source, no-code platform for web scraping, crawling, and AI data extraction, enabling users to quickly transform websites into structured APIs. It supports self-hosted deployment for greater control.
- [instaloader/instaloader](https://github.com/instaloader/instaloader) — ⭐ 12,785 · `Python` · CLI Tool — Instaloader is a Python CLI tool and library for downloading Instagram profiles, posts (pictures and videos), stories, and other associated metadata such as captions and geotags. It functions as a comprehensive Instagram media scraper.
- [gosom/google-maps-scraper](https://github.com/gosom/google-maps-scraper) — ⭐ 3,955 · `Go` · CLI Tool — This Go-based tool scrapes and extracts various business details from Google Maps, including names, addresses, phone numbers, websites, ratings, and reviews. It is designed for web scraping and data extraction from a specific online platform.
- [speedyapply/JobSpy](https://github.com/speedyapply/JobSpy) — ⭐ 3,430 · `Python` · Library — JobSpy is a Python library designed for scraping job postings from major platforms such as LinkedIn, Indeed, Glassdoor, Google Jobs, and ZipRecruiter. It enables users to automate the collection of job data for various purposes, including job search and market analysis.
- [any4ai/AnyCrawl](https://github.com/any4ai/AnyCrawl) — ⭐ 3,366 · `TypeScript` · CLI Tool — AnyCrawl is a Node.js/TypeScript web crawler tool designed to convert website content into LLM-friendly structured data. It also extracts structured SERP results from various search engines like Google and Bing, utilizing native multi-threading for bulk processing.
- [oxylabs/google-news-scraper](https://github.com/oxylabs/google-news-scraper) — ⭐ 2,881 · `Python` · Library — This repository provides a Python library to interact with the Google News Scraper API. It allows users to obtain the latest global news, including sources, headlines, URLs, and publication dates from the Google News platform for various projects.
- [lncrawl/lightnovel-crawler](https://github.com/lncrawl/lightnovel-crawler) — ⭐ 2,352 · `Python` · CLI Tool — This Python console application crawls online sources for light novels and web novels, then generates and downloads them as e-books, typically in EPUB format. It helps users create their own digital libraries of text content.
- [tavily-ai/tavily-mcp](https://github.com/tavily-ai/tavily-mcp) — ⭐ 2,202 · `JavaScript` · MCP Server — Tavily-mcp is a production-ready MCP (Multi-Modal Perception or similar) server providing real-time capabilities for searching, extracting, mapping, and crawling data.
- [watercrawl/WaterCrawl](https://github.com/watercrawl/WaterCrawl) — ⭐ 1,831 · `TypeScript` · Application — WaterCrawl is an open-source web crawler designed to transform raw web content into clean, structured, and LLM-ready data. It's built specifically to support RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) and AI agents by providing high-quality input data.
- [misiektoja/instagram\_monitor](https://github.com/misiektoja/instagram_monitor) — ⭐ 1,037 · `Python` · Application — This tool tracks Instagram users' activities, profile changes, and captures content, offering real-time monitoring with dashboards and instant notifications for social media intelligence.
- [orangecoding/fredy](https://github.com/orangecoding/fredy) — ⭐ 791 · `JavaScript` · Self-hosted Service — Fredy is a self-hosted web scraper that continuously searches German real estate platforms like ImmoScout24 for new apartment and house listings. It delivers instant alerts to users via multiple communication channels, simplifying the housing search process.
- [ni5arga/sightline](https://github.com/ni5arga/sightline) — ⭐ 434 · `TypeScript` · CLI Tool — Sightline is an OSINT search engine that allows AI to query OpenStreetMap data for real-world physical infrastructure like roads and buildings. It returns structured geographic information rather than web pages.
- [iBigQiang/feedgrab](https://github.com/iBigQiang/feedgrab) — ⭐ 357 · `Python` · CLI Tool — Feedgrab is a universal content grabbing tool that can fetch, normalize, and digest content from over seven platforms, including social media and video sites. It is designed to efficiently collect large volumes of data, such as thousands of tweets, while managing rate limits.
- [ScrapingBee/google-reviews-scraper](https://github.com/ScrapingBee/google-reviews-scraper) — ⭐ 226 · Library — This repository offers a scalable Google Reviews scraper and API integration for extracting structured ratings, review text, and business insights. It's designed for efficient data extraction and parsing of Google Maps reviews.
- [dondai1234/master-fetch](https://github.com/dondai1234/master-fetch) — ⭐ 215 · `Python` · MCP Server — Master-fetch is a self-hosted MCP server for web fetching and data scraping, featuring Cloudflare bypass, Trafilatura extraction, and smart routing. It's designed to provide AI agents with free access to the entire web, eliminating the need for expensive search APIs or data scraping subscriptions.
- [0xNyk/xint](https://github.com/0xNyk/xint) — ⭐ 209 · `TypeScript` · CLI Tool — This is a CLI tool providing intelligence capabilities for X/Twitter, enabling users to search, monitor, analyze, and engage on the platform. It functions as an AI agent skill and integrates with xAPI and xAI APIs.
- [Mohamedsaleh14/Reddit\_Scrapper](https://github.com/Mohamedsaleh14/Reddit_Scrapper) — ⭐ 167 · `Python` · Application — This Python project is a Reddit scraper that uses GPT models to identify marketing pain points from Reddit posts. It's designed to automate market research and provide insights for business growth.
- [Owlinkai/redroom](https://github.com/Owlinkai/redroom) — ⭐ 19 · `TypeScript` · Self-hosted Service — A real-time open-source intelligence (OSINT) platform designed to aggregate and process global news and information. It transforms fragmented data into actionable intelligence through real-time tracking and intelligent dashboards.
- [fluyeporlaweb/mcp-x-intelligence](https://github.com/fluyeporlaweb/mcp-x-intelligence) — ⭐ 17 · `TypeScript` · MCP Server — This is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that provides AI agents with research capabilities on X/Twitter data. It's designed to be compatible with tools like Claude, Cursor, and other MCP-enabled AI agents.
- [wevm/curl.md](https://github.com/wevm/curl.md) — ⭐ 10 · `TypeScript` · CLI Tool — Curl.md is a tool that converts content from a given URL into markdown format, designed to help AI agents get better context from web pages and save on token usage.

</details>

## Other

Dataset versioning, dedup, and the odds-and-ends that keep your data stack honest

<details>
<summary>Show 42 repos</summary>

- [nocodb/nocodb](https://github.com/nocodb/nocodb) — ⭐ 63,841 · `TypeScript` · Self-hosted Service — NocoDB is a free and self-hostable open-source alternative to Airtable, transforming databases into a spreadsheet-like interface. It functions as a no-code platform, allowing users to build applications and manage data easily without writing code.
- [666ghj/MiroFish](https://github.com/666ghj/MiroFish) — ⭐ 59,280 · `Python` · Library — MiroFish is a simple and universal swarm intelligence engine capable of predicting various outcomes. It leverages collective intelligence for general-purpose forecasting.
- [dbeaver/dbeaver](https://github.com/dbeaver/dbeaver) — ⭐ 50,513 · `Java` · Application — DBeaver is a free universal database tool and SQL client designed to connect to over 100 different databases. It provides a graphical user interface for database management and now includes AI capabilities to assist with tasks like query generation.
- [PostHog/posthog](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog) — ⭐ 34,396 · `Python` · Self-hosted Service — An all-in-one developer platform for building successful products, offering product and web analytics, session replay, error tracking, feature flags, experimentation, surveys, data warehousing, and an AI product assistant. It helps debug code, ship features faster, and centralize customer data.
- [plausible/analytics](https://github.com/plausible/analytics) — ⭐ 26,404 · `Elixir` · Self-hosted Service — Plausible Analytics is an open-source, privacy-first web analytics solution. It serves as a lightweight, cookie-free alternative to Google Analytics, offering both self-hosted and cloud deployment options for website statistics.
- [CodePhiliaX/Chat2DB](https://github.com/CodePhiliaX/Chat2DB) — ⭐ 25,564 · `Java` · Application — This is an AI-driven SQL client and database tool that supports over 16 different databases and can generate SQL queries using AI.
- [google-research/timesfm](https://github.com/google-research/timesfm) — ⭐ 19,417 · `Python` · Model Weights — TimesFM, a Time Series Foundation Model by Google Research, is a pretrained model specifically developed for time-series forecasting.
- [microsoft/data-formulator](https://github.com/microsoft/data-formulator) — ⭐ 15,255 · `TypeScript` · Application — This tool leverages artificial intelligence to facilitate the creation of rich and informative data visualizations. Its purpose is to transform data into compelling visual representations with AI assistance.
- [keplergl/kepler.gl](https://github.com/keplergl/kepler.gl) — ⭐ 11,898 · `TypeScript` · Application — Kepler.gl is an open-source geospatial analysis tool designed for visualizing large-scale geolocation datasets directly in the browser.
- [hasaneyldrm/exercises-dataset](https://github.com/hasaneyldrm/exercises-dataset) — ⭐ 11,562 · `HTML` · Dataset — This repository provides a comprehensive fitness dataset containing information for 1,324 exercises, including animation GIFs, thumbnails, muscle group and equipment data, and step-by-step instructions in multiple languages. It serves as the data layer for the LogPress fitness application.
- [growthbook/growthbook](https://github.com/growthbook/growthbook) — ⭐ 7,753 · `TypeScript` · Self-hosted Service — An open-source platform providing feature flags, experimentation tools (A/B testing), and product analytics capabilities. It integrates with various data warehouses for data-driven product development.
- [ScottPlot/ScottPlot](https://github.com/ScottPlot/ScottPlot) — ⭐ 6,556 · `C#` · Library — An interactive plotting library for .NET, enabling developers to create charts and visualize millions of data points within their applications.
- [glideapps/glide-data-grid](https://github.com/glideapps/glide-data-grid) — ⭐ 5,187 · `TypeScript` · Library — Glide Data Grid is an extremely fast React component designed for displaying millions of rows of data in a grid format within web applications. It offers rich rendering, first-class accessibility, and full TypeScript support for efficient data presentation.
- [Nixtla/neuralforecast](https://github.com/Nixtla/neuralforecast) — ⭐ 4,083 · `Python` · Library — This repository offers over 30 scalable and user-friendly neural forecasting algorithms implemented in Python. It's designed for machine learning practitioners and researchers working with time-series data and deep learning models for forecasting.
- [schemaspy/schemaspy](https://github.com/schemaspy/schemaspy) — ⭐ 3,589 · `HTML` · Application — This tool generates entity-relationship (ER) diagrams and comprehensive documentation from existing database schemas, simplifying the understanding and management of database structures. It helps users easily visualize and document their database designs.
- [slatedb/slatedb](https://github.com/slatedb/slatedb) — ⭐ 3,011 · `Rust` · Library — A cloud-native embedded storage engine built on object storage, designed to optimize data access latency and costs by intelligently probing for files.
- [GeostatsGuy/DataScienceInteractivePython](https://github.com/GeostatsGuy/DataScienceInteractivePython) — ⭐ 2,535 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Tutorial/Course — This repository offers interactive Python dashboards designed to help users learn data science concepts, including statistical methods like Bayesian updating.
- [graphistry/pygraphistry](https://github.com/graphistry/pygraphistry) — ⭐ 2,480 · `Python` · Library — PyGraphistry is a Python library for loading, shaping, embedding, and exploring large graphs using the GPU-accelerated Graphistry visual graph analyzer. It facilitates the visualization and analysis of massive graph datasets.
- [Rohithgilla12/data-peek](https://github.com/Rohithgilla12/data-peek) — ⭐ 1,585 · `TypeScript` · Application — Data Peek is a minimal and fast desktop application serving as a database client, allowing developers to quickly view and interact with their data. It also supports converting natural language queries into SQL and generating charts.
- [microsoft/Ontology-Playground](https://github.com/microsoft/Ontology-Playground) — ⭐ 1,415 · `TypeScript` · Application — An open-source web application for learning about and interacting with ontologies and Microsoft Fabric IQ. Users can explore pre-built ontologies, design their own visually, and export them as RDF/XML.
- [winedarksea/AutoTS](https://github.com/winedarksea/AutoTS) — ⭐ 1,398 · `Python` · Library — This project provides an automated tool for time series forecasting, utilizing machine learning, deep learning, and genetic algorithms to predict future values from historical data. It handles preprocessing and feature engineering for various time series datasets.
- [wannabespace/conar](https://github.com/wannabespace/conar) — ⭐ 1,355 · `TypeScript` · Application — Conar is an AI-powered SQL client that facilitates managing and interacting with various databases like Postgres, MySQL, MSSQL, and Clickhouse. It offers AI assistance to streamline database operations.
- [GONZOsint/geowifi](https://github.com/GONZOsint/geowifi) — ⭐ 1,288 · `Python` · CLI Tool — This Python CLI tool allows users to search for WiFi geolocation data using BSSID and SSID across various public databases. It is useful for open-source intelligence (OSINT) related to WiFi networks.
- [microsoft/flint-chart](https://github.com/microsoft/flint-chart) — ⭐ 1,279 · `TypeScript` · Framework — Flint is a visualization language designed for AI agents, enabling them to reliably generate high-quality charts from simple, human-editable specifications.
- [TabularisDB/tabularis](https://github.com/TabularisDB/tabularis) — ⭐ 1,276 · `TypeScript` · Application — Tabularis is a lightweight, cross-platform desktop client designed for developers to manage various databases like MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQLite. It offers a graphical interface, plugin support, and incorporates AI features for enhanced database interaction.
- [Zafer-Liu/Data-Analysis-Agent](https://github.com/Zafer-Liu/Data-Analysis-Agent) — ⭐ 1,155 · `Python` · AI Agent — This is an intelligent data analysis agent designed for business analysts, enabling them to ask natural language questions to analyze data, generate SQL queries, visualize results, and produce business insights and reports.
- [stardustai/dataset-viewer](https://github.com/stardustai/dataset-viewer) — ⭐ 785 · `TypeScript` · Application — This is a sleek dataset viewer application designed to stream and search massive datasets from various sources like S3, Hugging Face, WebDAV, or local files. It provides instant search capabilities for large datasets and was reportedly built using an AI agent.
- [Tablecruncher/tablecruncher](https://github.com/Tablecruncher/tablecruncher) — ⭐ 775 · `C++` · Application — A lightweight and powerful cross-platform CSV editor for macOS, Windows, and Linux, capable of handling large files with millions of rows and supporting built-in JavaScript macros for advanced data manipulation.
- [svg-project/flash-kmeans](https://github.com/svg-project/flash-kmeans) — ⭐ 585 · `Python` · Library — Flash-KMeans is a highly optimized, exact k-means clustering algorithm implementation designed for GPUs, offering significant speed improvements over existing GPU-based libraries like FAISS and NVIDIA cuML. It focuses on smart GPU dataflow for fast and memory-efficient exact k-means computations.
- [bruin-data/dac](https://github.com/bruin-data/dac) — ⭐ 527 · `Go` · Application — DaC is a dashboard-as-code tool that enables users to build interactive data dashboards using YAML and JSX. It includes a built-in semantic layer and can integrate with AI agents to create standardized, reviewable data visualizations.
- [TimeCopilot/timecopilot](https://github.com/TimeCopilot/timecopilot) — ⭐ 504 · `Python` · AI Agent — TimeCopilot is a GenAI forecasting agent built on LLMs and Time Series Foundation Models. It enables users to forecast, cross-validate, and detect anomalies using multiple foundation models through a single API, turning natural language queries into production-ready forecasts for various applications like finance and web analytics.
- [TutteInstitute/evoc](https://github.com/TutteInstitute/evoc) — ⭐ 302 · `Python` · Library — EVōC is a new Python algorithm and library for efficient clustering of embedding vectors. It is designed to provide high-quality clusters significantly faster than traditional methods, making it suitable for various data analysis and machine learning tasks.
- [opengeos/GeoLibre](https://github.com/opengeos/GeoLibre) — ⭐ 287 · `TypeScript` · Self-hosted Service — GeoLibre is a lightweight, cloud-native GIS platform designed for visualizing, exploring, and analyzing geospatial data across desktop, mobile, and web environments.
- [developmentseed/deck.gl-raster](https://github.com/developmentseed/deck.gl-raster) — ⭐ 183 · `TypeScript` · Library — A TypeScript library for client-side visualization of large geospatial raster datasets like GeoTIFF and Zarr in a web browser, leveraging WebGL2 and deck.gl for rendering.
- [next-1688/1688-item-select](https://github.com/next-1688/1688-item-select) — ⭐ 123 · `Python` · Application — This tool assists 1688 merchants in identifying high-priority products for operational focus. It uses a multi-dimensional scoring system and intelligent algorithms to recommend products, stratify them into tiers, and enable search based on various performance metrics.
- [zjunlp/DataMind](https://github.com/zjunlp/DataMind) — ⭐ 107 · `Python` · AI Agent — An open-source project providing LLM-based agents specifically designed to handle long-horizon and continuous data analysis tasks, addressing the limitations of traditional, episodic data analysis benchmarks.
- [colossus-lab/openarg\_backend](https://github.com/colossus-lab/openarg_backend) — ⭐ 103 · `Python` · Self-hosted Service — An AI-powered backend system to analyze Argentine government open data, featuring a multi-agent pipeline, data connectors, natural language to SQL capabilities, and LLMs for processing.
- [GoogleCloudPlatform/knowledge-catalog](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/knowledge-catalog) — ⭐ 64 · `Python` · Boilerplate/Template — This repository provides tools and sample code for Google Cloud's Knowledge Catalog, designed to help users manage and organize data and knowledge assets, with potential applications for AI agent memory systems.
- [rerun-io/examples-monorepo](https://github.com/rerun-io/examples-monorepo) — ⭐ 40 · `Python` · Tutorial/Course — This monorepo contains examples showcasing the Rerun SDK for logging and visualizing streams of multimodal data from robotics, computer vision, and machine learning systems, including demonstrations of headless rendering.
- [StewartLynch/SQLiteER](https://github.com/StewartLynch/SQLiteER) — ⭐ 31 · `Swift` · Application — A native macOS application that visualizes SQLite databases, including CoreData, SwiftData, and SQLiteData stores, as interactive entity-relationship diagrams which can be rearranged and exported.
- [Nonanti/narwhal](https://github.com/Nonanti/narwhal) — ⭐ 16 · `Rust` · MCP Server — A terminal-based database client that connects to multiple SQL databases like Postgres, MySQL, and SQLite. It features a built-in Model Context Protocol (MCP) server, allowing AI assistants like Claude Desktop or Cursor to directly query database schemas.
- [elastic/example-mcp-dashbuilder](https://github.com/elastic/example-mcp-dashbuilder) — ⭐ 11 · `TypeScript` · MCP Server — This is an example application for the Elastic Multimodal Conversation Platform (MCP) that enables users to build interactive Kibana dashboards within AI conversations. It queries Elasticsearch data using ES\|QL and automatically selects appropriate chart types.

</details>
